codeReview.summary: The implementation itself removes the raw-token refresh/reset fallbacks, retires `cid` emission from new JWTs, enforces canonical company-code binding, and leaves the deprecated superadmin forgot alias as a 410 tombstone with no product-side effects. The blocking gap is in the regression proof: the new cross-flow test that is supposed to prove `forgot -> reset -> login -> refresh -> /auth/me` still works bypasses the forgot-issued token by inserting its own digest row directly with `JdbcTemplate`, so it does not actually verify that the forgot path still produces a usable digest-backed reset token after the compatibility removal.

issues.description: `canonicalRecoveryFlow_forgotResetLoginRefreshAndMe_succeeds_withDigestBackedResetToken()` calls `postForgot(...)` and then manually inserts a digest row with `jdbcTemplate.update(...)` instead of consuming the reset token produced by the forgot flow. That leaves VAL-CROSS-001 / the feature's required canonical recovery proof unmet: the test would still pass even if `/auth/password/forgot` stopped persisting a usable digest-backed reset token after the compatibility-path removal.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared auth hardening note is now stale: it still documents startup backfill and raw-token fallback as active rollout behavior even though this feature retired both paths.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/auth-hardening.md:27-28 still says `AuthSecretStorageBackfillRunner` backfills legacy plaintext rows and runtime lookup/revoke/reset flows fall back to legacy raw-token columns, while commit 20ed8adc removes those fallbacks and turns `AuthSecretStorageBackfillRunner` into a no-op log-only runner.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The security-backend worker guidance says auth-surface changes that alter token or header contracts must update shared frontend guidance, but the packet left the frontend handoff stale after removing the `cid` JWT claim.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: security-backend-worker SKILL.md Step 5 requires updating `.factory/library/frontend-handoff.md` for touched auth/admin surface changes. Commit 20ed8adc removes `cid` emission in `erp-domain/src/main/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/core/security/JwtTokenService.java`, but `.factory/library/frontend-handoff.md:104` still says access tokens contain `cid`.

summary: FAIL. The product code looks aligned with canonical-only auth behavior, but the new recovery-flow regression test does not actually prove forgot-issued digest tokens still work because it injects its own token row after the forgot call.
